NOTE: secureworks.com 301-redirects to https://www.sophos.com/en-us/news/abrahams-ax-likely-linked-to-moses-staff (Secureworks→Sophos) — update source_url to the Sophos URL on intake; original returns a redirect, not a clean 200.

name: BlackByte ransomware exploits VMware ESXi CVE-2024-37085 (Cisco Talos) — Conti-linked RaaS
vendor_name_for_actor: BlackByte (Cisco Talos); Conti offshoot RaaS
notes: THN loads; confirms (per Cisco Talos) BlackByte likely exploiting VMware ESXi CVE-2024-37085, malicious 'ESX Admins' AD group, self-propagating wormable encryptor, BYOVD with four drivers, and 'one of the autonomous ransomware offshoots ... emerged ... leading up to shutdown of ... Conti.' 2nd BlackByte event (corpus has only 2022 SF 49ers). Conti-lineage = vendor assessment, hence strongly_inferred.

name: Emotet botnet returns and rebuilds via TrickBot infrastructure ('Operation Reacharound')
vendor_name_for_actor: Emotet operators / Mealybug / TA542 (Cryptolaemus, GData, Advanced Intel; corroborated by Lumen Black Lotus Labs)
attributing_org: Cryptolaemus research group, GData, Advanced Intel
notes: BleepingComputer loads; verbatim 'Emotet research group Cryptolaemus, GData, and Advanced Intel have begun to see the TrickBot malware dropping a loader for Emotet on infected devices,' i.e. the Nov-2021 rebuild ('Operation Reacharound') after the Jan-2021 takedown. 2nd Emotet event (corpus has 2020 pre-takedown peak; Jan-2021 Ladybird takedown sits under US slice). Distinct.

name: Stealth Falcon (UAE) Deadglyph backdoor espionage against a Middle East government entity
vendor_name_for_actor: Stealth Falcon (ESET); aka Project Raven / FruityArmor (UAE-linked per MITRE)
notes: ESET loads; verbatim 'we attribute Deadglyph with high confidence to the Stealth Falcon APT group' and 'Stealth Falcon (also known as Project Raven or FruityArmor) is a threat group linked to the United Arab Emirates according to MITRE,' Mideast govt victim, related sample from Qatar. Fills the AE Stealth Falcon gap (corpus has 2012/2016/2018/2025). Malware→actor is high-confidence; UAE-state nexus is via MITRE characterization, so strongly_inferred holds.

name: South Korean National Office of Investigation attributes 83 defense-contractor breaches to Lazarus, Kimsuky and Andariel
vendor_name_for_actor: Lazarus, Kimsuky (APT43), Andariel — named jointly by South Korea's National Office of Investigation / KNPA
attributing_org: South Korea National Office of Investigation (Korean National Police Agency, KNPA)
notes: The Record loads; ROK National Office of Investigation attributes infiltration of 83 defense companies/contractors (data stolen from ~10, Oct 2022–Jul 2023) to Lazarus, Kimsuky and Andariel; Kimsuky specifically exploited an email-server flaw at a subcontractor Apr–Jul 2023. Distinct from corpus AA24-207A (Jul-2024 Andariel) and all other Kimsuky entries; not in prior queue. Reviewer flag retained: file under KP (actor-state) vs cross-ref KR (attributing/victim state).

name: France (VIGINUM/SGDSN + Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs) publicly attributes the Storm-1516 information-manipulation campaign to Russia
vendor_name_for_actor: Storm-1516 (Microsoft naming; VIGINUM/SGDSN); linked to the Centre for Geopolitical Expertise (CGE); overlaps but distinct from Doppelganger/RRN
attributing_org: France — Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs + VIGINUM / SGDSN
notes: War on the Rocks loads; confirms VIGINUM's May 2025 assessment 'described the group [Storm-1516] as operating from Russia, with ties to the Kremlin' and that the French MFA condemned Russia's destabilizing activities. NEW actor in thin FR slice; distinct from corpus Portal Kombat / RRN-Doppelganger / Matriochka and not in prior queue. CAVEAT: this secondary source does NOT itself detail the election-targeting/anti-Ukraine specifics — intake should re-pull the SGDSN/VIGINUM PDF (20250507 Technical report Storm-1516) as primary and confirm the new-slug decision vs the existing Doppelganger/RRN actor.
